originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&irq` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE true)
     (= CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN true)
     (= CONFIG_OF_IRQ true))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF_IRQ () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 12: `int irq = *ops` inside irq_domain_add() (compiled when IRQ_DOMAIN=T). irq is assigned but never used after — dead store in all configurations where this function runs. The '// ERROR' at L12 marks the VBDB-known null dereference bug (when OF_IRQ=F, ops=NULL), but this DEAD_STORE alarm is a distinct new bug not in VBDB. The dead store requires only IRQ_DOMAIN=T (TWL4030=T internally defines IRQ_DOMAIN, so TWL4030=T AND IRQ_DOMAIN=T is equivalent to just TWL4030=T). OF_IRQ only affects whether ops is NULL; irq is dead regardless. PC requires OF_IRQ=T unnecessarily — over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_NEW_OVER.
